summary: No authentication or authorization mechanism found anywhere in the codebase

tests.description: Admin logs endpoint accessible without any token or credential
tests.actual_behavior: HTTP 200 — no auth check in route handler or middleware
tests.evidence: routes/admin.py line 15-30: handler has only `db: Session = Depends(get_db)` — no security dependency injected
tests.notes: Any unauthenticated user can read ALL scan logs including user input_data

tests.description: Admin stats endpoint accessible without any token or credential
tests.actual_behavior: HTTP 200 — no auth check in route handler or middleware
tests.evidence: routes/admin.py line 32-67: handler has only `db: Session = Depends(get_db)` — no security dependency
tests.notes: Exposes full threat analytics to any unauthenticated party

tests.description: CSV export of ALL scan logs accessible without credentials
tests.actual_behavior: HTTP 200 StreamingResponse with full CSV dump — no auth check
tests.evidence: routes/admin.py line 69-85: handler has only `db: Session = Depends(get_db)` — no security dependency
tests.notes: Returns all scan logs with input_data column — may contain sensitive user-submitted content (URLs, UPI IDs, OTP messages). Full database export exposed publicly.

tests.description: Analysis endpoints are intentionally public — no auth needed by design
tests.evidence: routes/url.py, screenshot.py, qr.py, otp.py, upi.py, voice.py — all use only db dependency
tests.notes: Public access is by design for these scan endpoints. Risk: rate-limiting abuse without throttling.

tests.description: No JWT / API key / session middleware registered anywhere in the application
tests.actual_behavior: main.py registers only CORSMiddleware — no authentication middleware present
tests.evidence: main.py lines 17-23: only CORSMiddleware added. No HTTPBearer, OAuth2, APIKeyHeader, or custom JWT middleware found anywhere
tests.notes: SECRET_KEY is defined in .env but never consumed — no JWT signing, verification, or middleware is implemented.

tests.description: SECRET_KEY in .env is defined but never imported or used in any code file
tests.actual_behavior: os.getenv('SECRET_KEY') is never called in any route, middleware, or utility
tests.evidence: Grep across entire backend: SECRET_KEY appears only in .env and .env.example — zero references in Python files
tests.notes: A SECRET_KEY suggests JWT or session signing was planned but never implemented. The key is also weak ('change-me-in-production') and committed to plaintext .env.
